sunnah_ref_en: Ka'b bin Malik (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) set out on Thursday for the expedition of Tabuk. He liked to set out on a journey on Thursdays.
iwh_en: Ka'b bin Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ set out on Thursday for the expedition of Tabuk. He liked to set out on a journey on Thursdays.
iwh_id: Ka'b bin Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ set out on Thursday for the expedition of Tabuk. He liked to set out on a journey on Thursdays.

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعن صخر بن وداعة الغامدي الصحابي رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ “اللهم بارك لأمتي في بكورها‏"‏ وكان إذا بعث سرية أو جيشاً بعثهم من أول النهار‏.‏ وكان صخر تاجراً فكان يبعث تجارته أول النهار، فأثري وكثر ماله” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Sakhr bin Wada'ah Al-Ghamidi (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "O Allah! Bless my people in the early part of the day (morning). Whenever he dispatched a detachment or an army-unit, he would dispatch it at the beginning of the day (soon after dawn). The narrator, Sakhr (May Allah be pleased with him) was a merchant, and he used to send off his merchandise at the beginning of the day. So his trade flourished and he made a good fortune..
iwh_en: Sakhr bin Wada'ah Al-Ghamidi (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "O Allah! Bless my people in the early part of the day (morning). Whenever he dispatched a detachment or an army-unit, he would dispatch it at the beginning of the day (soon after dawn). The narrator, Sakhr (may Allah be pleased with him) was a merchant, and he used to send off his merchandise at the beginning of the day. So his trade flourished and he made a good fortune..
iwh_id: Sakhr bin Wada'ah Al-Ghamidi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "Ya Allah! Bless my orang-orang di early part dari day (morning). Setiap kali he dispatched a detachment or an army-unit, ia dispatch it at the beginning dari day (soon setelah dawn). The narrator, Sakhr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) was a merchant, dan ia biasa send off his merchandise at the beginning dari day. Jadi his trade flourished dan ia membuat a baik fortune..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: عن ابن عمر رضي الله عنهما قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏لو أن الناس يعلمون من الوحدة ما أعلم ما سار راكب بليل وحده” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Were people to know of what I know about the dangers of travelling alone, no rider would travel alone at night.".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Were people to know of what I know about the dangers of travelling alone, no rider would travel alone at night.".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Were orang-orang to mengetahui of what I mengetahui tentang the dangers of travelling alone, no rider would travel alone at night.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعن عمر بن شعيب عن أبيه عن جده رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ “الراكب شيطان والراكبان شيطانان والثلاثة ركب‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي والنسائي بأسانيد صحيحة، وقال الترمذي‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: 'Amr bin Shu'aib (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "A single rider is (accompanied with) Satan and two riders are (accompanied with) two Satans. Three riders form a group.".
iwh_en: 'Amr bin Shu'aib (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "A single rider is (accompanied with) Satan and two riders are (accompanied with) two Satans. Three riders form a group.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعن أبي سعيد وأبي هريرة رضي الله عنهما قالا‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ‏:‏ “إذا خرج ثلاثة في سفر فليأمروا أحدهم‏"‏ حديث حسن، ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "When three persons set out on a journey, they should appoint one of them as their leader.".
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "When three persons set out on a journey, they should appoint one of them as their leader.".
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "When three orang-orang set out on a journey, they should appoint one of them as their leader.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعن ابن عباس رضي الله عنهما عن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم   قال‏:‏ “خير الصحابة أربعة وخير السرايا أربعمائة وخير الجيوش أربعة آلاف ولن يغلب اثنا عشر ألفاً عن قلة‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The best number of companions is four; the best detachment is four hundred and the best army is four thousand; and twelve thousand men will not be defeated as a result of smallness of number.".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "The best number of companions is four; the best detachment is four hundred and the best army is four thousand; and twelve thousand men will not be defeated as a result of smallness of number.".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "The best number of companions is four; the best detachment is four hundred dan the best army is four thousand; dan twelve thousand men tidak akan be defeated as a result of smallness of number.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
عن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ‏:‏ “إذا سافرتم في الخصب فأعطوا الإبل حظها من الأرض وإذا سافرتم في الجدب فأسرعوا عليها السير وبادروا بها نقيها، وإذا عرستم، فاجتنبوا الطريق، فإن طرق الدواب، ومأوي الهوام بالليل” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
معنى‏:‏ “أعطوا الإبل حظها من الأرض” أي‏:‏ ارفقوا بها في السير لترعي في حال سيرها وقوله‏:‏ ‏ ‏نقيها‏ ‏ وهو بكسر النون، وإسكان القاف، وبالياء المثناة من تحت وهو‏:‏ المخ، معناه‏:‏ أسرعوا بها حتي تصلوا المقصد قبل أن يذهب مخها من ضنك السير‏.‏ و‏ ‏التعريس‏ ‏ النزول في الليل‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: "When you travel through green and lush land, give the camels their due from the ground, and when you travel through a dry and barren land, make them walk quickly lest their strength falters. When you encamp at night, keep away from the roads, for beasts pass there and they are the resort of insects at night.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When you travel through green and lush land, give the camels their due from the ground, and when you travel through a dry and barren land, make them walk quickly lest their strength falters. When you encamp at night, keep away from the roads, for beasts pass there and they are the resort of insects at night.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "When you travel melalui green dan lush land, memberikan the camels their due from the ground, and when you travel melalui a dry dan barren land, membuat them berjalan quickly lest their strength falters. When you encamp at night, menjaga away from the roads, for beasts pass there dan mereka are the resort of insects at night.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن أبي قتادة، رضي الله عنه، قال‏:‏ كان رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، إذا كان في سفر، فعرس بليل اضطجع علي يمينه وإذا عرس قبيل الصبح نصب ذراعه، ووضع رأسه علي كفه‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
قال العلماء‏:‏ إنما نصب ذراعه لئلا يستغرق في النوم، فتفوته صلاة الصبح عن وقتها أو عن أول وقتها‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Qatadah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) encamped at night during a journey, he would lie down on his right side, and when he stayed a little before dawn he would raise his forearm and place his head on the palm of his hand..
iwh_en: Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ encamped at night during a journey, he would lie down on his right side, and when he stayed a little before dawn he would raise his forearm and place his head on the palm of his hand..
iwh_id: Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ encamped at night dalam perjalanan, ia lie down on his kanan side, and ketika ia stayed a little sebelum dawn ia raise his forearm dan place his head di palm of his hand..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
عن أنس، رضي الله عنه، قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏عليكم بالدلجة، فإن الأرض تطوي بالليل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏
الدلجة‏ ‏ ‏:‏ السير في الليل‏

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Keep to travelling by night because the earth is folded (traversed more easily) during the night.".
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Keep to travelling by night because the earth is folded (traversed more easily) during the night.".
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Keep to travelling by night because the earth is folded (traversed more easily) selama the night.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن أبي ثعلبة الخشني، رضي الله عنه، قال‏:‏ كان الناس إذا نزلوا منزلاً تفرقوا في الشعاب والأودية‏.‏ فقال رسول الله، صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏إن تفرقكم في هذه الشعاب والأودية إنما ذلكم من الشيطان‏!‏‏"‏ فلم ينزلوا بعد ذلك منزلا إلا انضم بعضهم إلي بعض‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Tha'labah Al-Khushani (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Whenever the people encamped during the journey, they would disperse in the mountain passes and valleys. (When) the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) noticed this he said, "Your act of scattering in the mountain passes and valleys is from Satan." Afterwards the Companions kept close together whenever they encamped..
iwh_en: Abu Tha'labah Al-Khushani (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that whenever the people encamped during the journey, they would disperse in the mountain passes and valleys. (When) the Messenger of Allah ﷺ noticed this he said, "Your act of scattering in the mountain passes and valleys is from Satan." Afterwards the Companions kept close together whenever they encamped..
iwh_id: Abu Tha'labah Al-Khushani (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa whenever the orang-orang encamped selama the journey, they would disperse in the mountain passes dan valleys. (When) Rasulullah ﷺ noticed this he bersabda, "Your act of scattering in the mountain passes dan valleys is from Satan." Kemudian the Companions menjaga close together whenever they encamped..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling

وعن سهل بن عمرو -وقيل سهل بن الربيع بن عمرو الأنصاري المعروف بابن الحنظلية، وهو من أهل بيعة الرضوان، رضي الله عنه، قال‏:‏ مر رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ببعير قد لحق ظهره بطنه، فقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏اتقوا الله في هذه البهائهم المعجمة، فاركبوها صالحة وكلوها صالحة‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Sahl bin 'Amr known as Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) happened to pass by a camel whose belly was sticking to its back (because of hunger), whereupon he said, "Fear Allah in respect of these dumb (animals). Ride them while they are fit, and slaughter them and eat their meat when they are fit.".
iwh_en: Sahl bin 'Amr known as Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ happened to pass by a camel whose belly was sticking to its back (because of hunger), whereupon he said, "Fear Allah in respect of these dumb (animals). Ride them while they are fit, and slaughter them and eat their meat when they are fit.".
iwh_id: Sahl bin 'Amr known as Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ happened to pass oleh camel whose belly was sticking to its back (because of hunger), whereupon he berkata, "Fear Allah in respect of these dumb (animals). Ride them sementara they are fit, and slaughter them dan eat their meat ketika mereka are fit.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن أبي جعفر عبد الله بن جعفر، رضي الله عنهما، قال‏:‏ أردفني رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ، ذات يوم خلفه، وأسر إلي حديثاً لا أحدث به أحداً من الناس وكان أحب ما أستتر به رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  لحاجته هدف أو حائش نخل‏.‏ يعنى‏:‏ حائط نخل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه هكذا مختصراً‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
وزاد فيه البرقاني بإسناد مسلم بعد قوله‏:‏ حائش نخل‏:‏ فدخل حائطاً لرجل من الأنصار، فإذا فيه جمل، فلما رأي رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  جرجر وذرفت عيناه، فأتاه النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم ، فمسح سراته -أى‏:‏ سنامه- وذفراه فسكن؛ فقال‏:‏ ‏ ‏من رب هذا الجمل، لمن هذا الجمل‏؟‏‏ ‏ فجاء فتى من الأنصار، فقال‏:‏ هذا لي يا رسول الله، فقال‏:‏ ‏ ‏أفلا تتقي الله في هذه البهيمة التي ملكك الله إياها‏؟‏ فإنه يشكو إلي أنك تجيعه وتدئبه‏ ‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود كرواية البرقاني‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
قوله‏:‏ ‏ ‏ذفراه‏ ‏ وهو بكسر الذال المعجمة وإسكان الفاء، وهو لفظ مفرد مؤنث‏.‏ قال أهل اللغة‏:‏ الذفري‏:‏ الموضع الذي يعرق من البعير خلف الأذن، وقوله‏:‏ ‏ ‏تذئبه‏ ‏ أي‏:‏ تتعبه‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Ja'far 'Abdullah bin Ja'far (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) made me ride behind him one day and confided something to me which I shall never disclose to anyone. ('Abdullah added:) He ((ﷺ) preferred to screen himself from others when responding to the call of nature, either behind a wall or the trunk of a date-palm..
iwh_en: Abu Ja'far 'Abdullah bin Ja'far (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ made me ride behind him one day and confided something to me which I shall never disclose to anyone. ('Abdullah added:) He ((ﷺ) preferred to screen himself from others when responding to the call of nature, either behind a wall or the trunk of a date-palm..
iwh_id: Abu Ja'far 'Abdullah bin Ja'far (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ membuat me ride di belakang him one day dan confided something to me which I shall never disclose to anyone. ('Abdullah added:) He ((ﷺ) preferred to screen himself from others when responding to the call of nature, either di belakang a wall or the trunk of a date-palm..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن أنس، رضي الله عنه، قال‏:‏ كنا إذا نزلنا منزلا لا نسبح حتي نحل الرحال‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد علي شرط مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
وقوله‏:‏ ‏ ‏لا نسبح‏ ‏‏:‏ أي لا نصلي  النافلة، ومعناه‏:‏ أنا -مع حرصنا علي الصلاة- لا نقدمها على حط الرحال وإراحة الدواب

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When we encamped, we would not offer (voluntary) prayers until we had unsaddled our beasts..
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that when we encamped, we would not offer (voluntary) prayers until we had unsaddled our beasts..
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa ketika kami encamped, kami not offer (voluntary) prayers hingga we had unsaddled our beasts..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري، رضي الله عنه، قال‏:‏ بينما نحن في سفر إذ جاء رجل علي راحلة له، فجعل يصرف بصره يميناً وشمالاً، فقال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏من كان معه فضل ظهر؛ فليعد به علي من لا ظهر له، ومن كان له فضل زاد فليعد به علي من لا زاد له‏"‏ فذكر من أصناف المال ما ذكره، حتي رأينا‏:‏ أنه لا حق لأحد منا في فضل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري، رضي الله عنه، قال: بينما نحن في سفر إذ جاء رجل علي راحلة له، فجعل يصرف بصره يميناً وشمالاً، فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم : "من كان معه فضل ظهر؛ فليعد به علي من لا ظهر له، ومن كان له فضل زاد فليعد به علي من لا زاد له" فذكر من أصناف المال ما ذكره، حتي رأينا: أنه لا حق لأحد منا في فضل" ((رواه مسلم)).

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: While we were travelling with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), a rider came and began to stare on the right and on the left. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who has an extra mount should hand it over to one who has none, and he who possesses surplus provision, should give it to him who is without provision." He named various kinds of possessions until we began to think (realize) that none of us had any right to anything surplus..
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that while we were travelling with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, a rider came and began to stare on the right and on the left. the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who has an extra mount should hand it over to one who has none, and he who possesses surplus provision, should give it to him who is without provision." He named various kinds of possessions until we began to think (realize) that none of us had any right to anything surplus..
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa while we were travelling with Rasulullah ﷺ, a rider came dan began to stare on the kanan dan on the kiri. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who has an extra mount should hand it over to one who has none, dan ia who possesses surplus provision, should memberikan it to him who is tanpa provision." He named various kinds of possessions hingga we began to berpikir (realize) that none of us had any kanan to anything surplus..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعن جابر رضي الله عنه، عن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، أنه أراد أن يغزو، فقال‏:‏ يا معشر المهاجرين والأنصار‏!‏ إن من إخوانكم قوماً، ليس لهم مال، ولا عشيرة، فليضم أحدكم إليه الرجلين، أو الثلاثة، فما لأحدنا من ظهر يحمله إلا كعقبة، يعني أحدهم‏.‏ قال‏:‏ فضممت إلي اثنين أو ثلاثة مالي إلا عقبة كعقبة أحدهم من جملي‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Jabir (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) made up his mind to go in an expedition, he would say to us, "O Muhajirin and the Ansar! There are among you such people who have no property and no kinsfolk. Let everyone of you take along with him two or three men. None of us had a spare animal we took rides by turn." So I took two or three men with me and I rode my camel by turn equally with them..

So I took two or three men with me and I rode my camel by turn equally with them..

There are di antara you such orang-orang yang have no property dan no kinsfolk. Let everyone of you mengambil along with him two or three men. None of us had a spare animal we membawa rides by turn." Jadi I membawa two or three men with me dan aku rode my camel by turn equally with them..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعنه قال‏:‏ كان رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  يتخلف في المسير، فيزجي الضعيف ويردف ويدعو له، ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن ابن عمر، رضي الله عنهما، أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  كان إذا استوى علي بعيره خارجاً إلى سفر، كبر ثلاثاً، ثم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏سبحان الذي سخر لنا هذا وما كنا له مقرنين، وإنا إلي ربنا لمنقلبون‏.‏ اللهم إنا نسألك في سفرنا هذا البر والتقوى، ومن العمل ما ترضي‏.‏ اللهم هون علينا سفرنا هذا واطو عنا بعده‏.‏ اللهم أنت الصاحب في السفر والخليفة في الأهل‏.‏ اللهم إني أعوذ بك من وعثاء السفر وكآبة المنظر وسوء المنقلب في الأهل والمال والولد‏"‏ وإذا رجع قالهن وزاد فيهن‏:‏ ‏"‏آيبون تائبون عابدون لربنا حامدون‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
معنى ‏ ‏مقرنين‏ ‏‏:‏ مطيقين‏.‏ ‏ ‏والوعثاء‏ ‏ بفتح الواو وإسكان العين المهملة وبالثاء المثلثة وبالمد، وهي‏:‏ الشدة‏.‏ و‏ ‏الكآبة‏ ‏ بالمد، وهي‏:‏ تغير النفس من حزن ونحوه‏.‏ ‏ ‏والمنقلب‏ ‏‏:‏ المرجع‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: Whenever the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) mounted his camel for setting out on a journey, he would recite: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)," thrice. Then he (ﷺ) would supplicate: "Subhanal-ladhi sakh-khara lana hadha, wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina lamunqalibun. Allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadh al-birra wat-taqwa, wa minal-'amali ma tarda. Allahumma hawwin 'alaina safarana hadha, watwi 'anna bu'dahu. Allahumma Antas-Sahibu fissafari, wal-Khalifatu fil- ahli. Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'ta'is-safari, wa kaabatil- manzari, wa su'il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahli wal-waladi (Far removed from imperfection is the One Who has made this subservient to us, for we have no power to subjugate it, and certainly to our Rubb shall we return. O Allah, we ask You during this journey of ours for righteousness, piety and such deeds as are pleasing to You. O Allah, make easy for us this journey of ours and make the distance short for us. O Allah, You are our Companion during the journey and the Guardian of the family and the property in our absence. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardships of travelling, unhappiness connected with ghastly scenes and evil turns in property and family)." When he returned, he recited this supplication making addition of these words: "Ayibuna, ta'ibuna, 'abiduna, li-Rabbina hamidun (We are those who return; those who repent; those who worship and those who praise our Rubb).".

Allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadh al-birra wat-taqwa, wa minal-'amali ma tarda. Allahumma hawwin 'alaina safarana hadha, watwi 'anna bu'dahu. Allahumma Antas-Sahibu fissafari, wal-Khalifatu fil- ahli. Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'ta'is-safari, wa kaabatil- manzari, wa su'il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahli wal-waladi (Far removed from imperfection is the One Who has made this subservient to us, for we have no power to subjugate it, and certainly to our Rubb shall we return. O Allah, we ask You during this journey of ours for righteousness, piety and such deeds as are pleasing to You. O Allah, make easy for us this journey of ours and make the distance short for us. O Allah, You are our Companion during the journey and the Guardian of the family and the property in our absence. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardships of travelling, unhappiness connected with ghastly scenes and evil turns in property and family)." When he returned, he recited this supplication making addition of these words: "Ayibuna, ta'ibuna, 'abiduna, li-Rabbina hamidun (We are those who return; those who repent; those who worship and those who praise our Rubb).".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa setiap kali Rasulullah ﷺ mounted his camel for setting out on a journey, ia membaca: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)," thrice. Kemudian he (ﷺ) would supplicate: "Subhanal-ladhi sakh-khara lana hadha, wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina lamunqalibun. Allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadh al-birra wat-taqwa, wa minal-'amali ma tarda. Allahumma hawwin 'alaina safarana hadha, watwi 'anna bu'dahu. Allahumma Antas-Sahibu fissafari, wal-Khalifatu fil- ahli. Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'ta'is-safari, wa kaabatil- manzari, wa su'il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahli wal-waladi (Far removed from imperfection is the One Who has membuat this subservient to us, for we have no power to subjugate it, and certainly to our Rubb shall we return. Ya Allah, we ask You selama this journey of ours for righteousness, piety dan such deeds as are pleasing to You. Ya Allah, membuat easy for us this journey of ours dan membuat the distance pendek for us. Ya Allah, You are our Companion selama the journey dan the Guardian dari family dan the property tanpa kehadiran kami. Ya Allah, Saya berlindung in You dari hardships of travelling, unhappiness connected with ghastly scenes dan evil turns in property dan family)." Ketika ia returned, he recited this supplication making addition of these words: "Ayibuna, ta'ibuna, 'abiduna, li-Rabbina hamidun (We are those yang return; those yang repent; those yang worship dan those yang praise our Rubb).".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن عبد الله بن سرجس، رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كان رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  إذا سافر يتعوذ من وعثاء السفر، وكآبة المنقلب، والحور بعد الكون، ودعوة المظلوم‏.‏ وسوء المنظر في الأهل والمال‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏
هكذا هو في صحيح مسلم‏:‏ الحور بعد الكون، بالنون، وكذا رواه الترمذي والنسائي‏.‏ قال الترمذي‏:‏ يروي ‏:‏الكور‏ ‏ بالراء، وكلاهما له وجه‏.‏   قال العلماء‏:‏ ومعناه بالنون والراء جميعاً‏:‏ الرجوع من الاستقامة أو الزيادة إلي النقص‏.‏ قالوا‏:‏ ورواية الراء مأخوذة من تكوير العمامة، وهو لفها وجمعها، ورواية النون، من الكون، مصدراً ‏ ‏كان يكون كوناً‏ ‏ إذا وجد واستقر‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: 'Abdullah bin Sarjis (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Whenever the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) proceeded on a journey, he would seek refuge in Allah from the hardships of the journey, and against deviation after guidance, and against the supplication of the oppressed, and occurrences of unpleasant events in the family and property. He would say: "Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'tha'is- safari, wa kaabatil-munqalabi, wal-hauri ba'dal-kauni, wa da'watil- mazlumi, wa su'il-manzari fil-ahli wal- mal.".
iwh_en: 'Abdullah bin Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: Whenever the Messenger of Allah ﷺ proceeded on a journey, he would seek refuge in Allah from the hardships of the journey, and against deviation after guidance, and against the supplication of the oppressed, and occurrences of unpleasant events in the family and property. He would say: "Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'tha'is- safari, wa kaabatil-munqalabi, wal-hauri ba'dal-kauni, wa da'watil- mazlumi, wa su'il-manzari fil-ahli wal- mal.".
iwh_id:
'Abdullah bin Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan:
Setiap kali Rasulullah ﷺ proceeded on a journey, ia berlindung in Allah dari hardships dari journey, and terhadap deviation setelah guidance, and terhadap the supplication dari oppressed, and occurrences of unpleasant events di family dan property. Beliau say: "Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'tha'is- safari, wa kaabatil-munqalabi, wal-hauri ba'dal-kauni, wa da'watil- mazlumi, wa su'il-manzari fil-ahli wal- mal.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن علي بن ربيعة قال‏:‏ شهدت علي بن أبي طالب رضي الله عنه أتي بدابة ليركبها، فلما وضع رجله في الركاب قال‏:‏ بسم الله، فلما استوي علي ظهرها قال‏:‏ الحمد لله الذي سخر لنا هذا، وما كنا له مقرنين، وإنا إلي ربنا لمنقلبون، ثم قال‏:‏ الحمد الله، ثلاث مرات، ثم قال‏:‏ الله اكبر ثلاث مرات، ثم قال‏:‏ سبحانك إني ظلمت نفسي فاغفر لي فإنه لا يغفر الذنوب إلا أنت، ثم ضحك، فقيل‏:‏ يا أمير المؤمنين من أي شئ ضحكت‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ رأيت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم فعل كما فعلت، ثم ضحك، فقلت‏:‏ يا رسول الله من أي شئ ضحكت‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ “إن ربك سبحانه يعجب من عبده إذا قال‏:‏ اغفر لي ذنوبي، يعلم أنه لا يغفر الذنوب غيره‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏ 
‏وفي بعض النسخ‏:‏ حديث صحيح‏.‏ وهذا لفظ أبي داود

sunnah_ref_en: 'Ali bin Rabi'ah reported: In my presence, a beast was brought to 'Ali bin Abu Talib (May Allah be pleased with him) for riding. When he put his foot in the stirrup, he said: "Bismillah (With the Name of Allah)." When he had settled himself on its back he recited: "Al-hamdu lillahil-ladhi sakh-khara lana hadha, wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina lamunqalibun. (All praise belongs to Allah Who has made this subservient to us, for we had not the strength to overpower it; and to our Rubb shall we return)." He then recited thrice: "Alhamdu lillah (Praise be to Allah)," and then three times: "Allahu Abkar (Allah is Greatest)." Then he said: "Subhanaka inni zalamtu nafsi faghfir li, innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa Anta (You are far removed from imperfection I have wronged myself, so forgive me, because none but You can forgive sins)." Then he smiled. It was asked: "Why have you smiled, O Amir Al-Mu'minin (Leader of the Believers)?" He replied: "I saw the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) doing as I have done. I (i.e., Ali) asked him (the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ)) the reason for smiling. He (ﷺ) said, 'Your Rubb, Glorious is He, is pleased when His slave seeks His forgiveness. He (the slave) has firm faith that none except Allah Alone can forgive sins)'.".

He then recited thrice: "Alhamdu lillah (Praise be to Allah)," and then three times: "Allahu Abkar (Allah is Greatest)." Then he said: "Subhanaka inni zalamtu nafsi faghfir li, innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa Anta (You are far removed from imperfection I have wronged myself, so forgive me, because none but You can forgive sins)." Then he smiled. It was asked: "Why have you smiled, O Amir Al-Mu'minin (Leader of the Believers)?" He replied: "I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ doing as I have done. I (i.e., Ali) asked him (the Messenger of Allah ﷺ) the reason for smiling. He (ﷺ) said, 'Your Rubb, Glorious is He, is pleased when His slave seeks His forgiveness. He (the slave) has firm faith that none except Allah Alone can forgive sins)'.".
iwh_id:
'Ali bin Rabi'ah meriwayatkan:
In my presence, a beast was membawa to 'Ali bin Abu Talib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) for riding. Ketika ia meletakkan his foot di stirrup, he berkata: "Bismillah (With the Name of Allah)." Ketika ia had settled himself on its back he recited: "Al-hamdu lillahil-ladhi sakh-khara lana hadha, wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina lamunqalibun. (All praise belongs to Allah Who has membuat this subservient to us, for we belum the strength to overpower it; dan to our Rubb shall we return)." He kemudian recited thrice: "Alhamdu lillah (Praise be to Allah)," and kemudian three times: "Allahu Abkar (Allah is Greatest)." Kemudian he berkata: "Subhanaka inni zalamtu nafsi faghfir li, innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa Anta (You are far removed from imperfection I have wronged myself, jadi forgive me, because none namun You can forgive sins)." Kemudian he smiled. It was bertanya: "Why have you smiled, O Amir Al-Mu'minin (Leader dari Believers)?" Ia menjawab: "Saya melihat Rasulullah ﷺ doing as I have done. I (i.e., Ali) bertanya him (Rasulullah ﷺ) the reason for smiling. He (ﷺ) berkata, 'Your Rubb, Glorious is He, is pleased when hamba-Nya seeks His ampunan. He (the slave) has firm iman that none except Allah Alone can forgive sins)'.".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: عن جابر رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كنا إذا صعدنا كبرنا، وإذا نزلنا سبحنا‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Jabir (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When we ascended (a height) we would proclaim: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)," and when we descended (from the height) we would proclaim: "Subhan Allah (Allah is free from imperfection)."'.
iwh_en: Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that when we ascended (a height) we would proclaim: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)," and when we descended (from the height) we would proclaim: "Subhan Allah (Allah is free from imperfection)."'.
iwh_id: Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa ketika kami ascended (a height) kami proclaim: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)," and ketika kami descended (dari height) kami proclaim: "Subhan Allah (Allah is free from imperfection)."'.

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) and his army ascended a height, they would proclaim: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)," and when they climbed down, they would proclaim: "Subhan Allah (Allah is free from imperfection).".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that whenever the Prophet ﷺ and his army ascended a height, they would proclaim: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)," and when they climbed down, they would proclaim: "Subhan Allah (Allah is free from imperfection).".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa setiap kali Nabi ﷺ dan his army ascended a height, mereka proclaim: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)," and ketika mereka climbed down, mereka proclaim: "Subhan Allah (Allah is free from imperfection).".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعنه قال كان النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم  إذا قفل من الحج أو العمرة كلما أوفى علي ثنيه أو فدفد كبر ثلاثاً، ثم قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏ لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له، له الملك وله الحمد وهو علي كل شئ قدير آيبون تائبون عابدون ساجدون لربنا حامدون‏.‏ صدق الله وعده، ونصر عبده، وهزم الأحزاب وحده‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏
وفي رواية لمسلم‏:‏ إذا قفل من الجيوش أو السرايا أو الحج أو العمرة
قوله‏:‏ ‏ ‏أوفى‏ ‏ أي‏:‏ ارتفع، وقوله‏:‏ ‏ ‏فدفد‏ ‏ هو بفتح الفاءين بينهما دال مهملة ساكنة وآخره دال أخرى وهو ‏:‏ الغليظ المرتفع من الأرض‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) returned from Hajj or 'Umrah and ascended a height or hill, he would recite thrice: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)." Then he would say, "La ilaha ill-allahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahul- mulku, wa lahul-hamdu, wa Huwa 'ala kulli shay'in Qadeer. Ayibuna ta'ibuna 'abiduna li-Rabbina hamidun. Sadaq-Allahu wa'dahu, wa nasara 'abdahu, wa hazamal-ahzaba wahdahu .
iwh_en: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that whenever the Prophet ﷺ returned from Hajj or 'Umrah and ascended a height or hill, he would recite thrice: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)." Then he would say, "La ilaha ill-allahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahul- mulku, wa lahul-hamdu, wa Huwa 'ala kulli shay'in Qadeer. Ayibuna ta'ibuna 'abiduna li-Rabbina hamidun. Sadaq-Allahu wa'dahu, wa nasara 'abdahu, wa hazamal-ahzaba wahdahu .
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa setiap kali Nabi ﷺ returned from Hajj or 'Umrah dan ascended a height or hill, ia membaca thrice: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)." Kemudian ia berkata, "La ilaha ill-allahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahul- mulku, wa lahul-hamdu, wa Huwa 'ala kulli shay'in Qadeer. Ayibuna ta'ibuna 'abiduna li-Rabbina hamidun. Sadaq-Allahu wa'dahu, wa nasara 'abdahu, wa hazamal-ahzaba wahdahu .

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن رجلاً قال‏:‏ يا رسول الله ، إني أريد أن أسافر فأوصني، قال‏:‏ ‏"‏عليك بتقوى الله، والتكبيرعلي كل شرف‏"‏ فلما ولى الرجل قال‏:‏ ‏"‏ اللهم اطو له البعد، وهون عليه السفر” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن رجلاً قال: يا رسول الله ، إني أريد أن أسافر فأوصني، قال: "عليك بتقوى الله، والتكبيرعلي كل شرف" فلما ولى الرجل قال: " اللهم اطو له البعد، وهون عليه السفر” ((رواه الترمذي وقال حديث حسن)).
isnad_ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن رجلاً قال: يا رسول الله

matn_ar_clean: إني أريد أن أسافر فأوصني، قال: "عليك بتقوى الله، والتكبيرعلي كل شرف" فلما ولى الرجل قال: " اللهم اطو له البعد، وهون عليه السفر” ((رواه الترمذي وقال حديث حسن)).
sunnah_ref_en:
Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man said: "O Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), I intend to set out on a journey, so counsel me." He (ﷺ) said, "Fear Allah, and ascending towards the raised ground, you should recite: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)." When the man turned away, he (ﷺ) said, "O Allah! Shorten the distance for him and make the journey easy for him."

.
iwh_en:
Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man said: "O Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), I intend to set out on a journey, so counsel me." He (ﷺ) said, "Fear Allah, and ascending towards the raised ground, you should recite: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)." When the man turned away, he (ﷺ) said, "O Allah! Shorten the distance for him and make the journey easy for him."

.
iwh_id:
Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa seorang laki-laki bersabda: "O Rasulullah ﷺ, I intend to set out on a journey, jadi counsel me." He (ﷺ) bersabda, "Fear Allah, and ascending kepada the raised ground, you should membaca: "Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greatest)." When the man berpaling, he (ﷺ) bersabda, "Ya Allah! Shorten the distance for him dan membuat the journey easy for him."

.

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن أبي موسي الأشعري رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كنا مع النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم في سفر، فكنا إذا أشرفنا على واد هللنا وكبرنا وارتفعت أصواتنا، فقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم   ‏:‏”يا أيها الناس أربعوا عل أنفسكم فإنكم لا تدعون أصم ولا غائباً‏.‏ إنه معكم ، إنه سميع قريب‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏
أربعوا” بفتح الباء الموحدة أي ‏:‏ أرفقوا بأنفسكم‏.

sunnah_ref_en: Khaulah bint Hakim (May Allah be pleased with her) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying, "Whosoever alights somewhere and says: 'A'udhu bikalimat-illahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaqa (I seek refuge with the Perfect Words of Allah from the evil of what He has created),' nothing will harm him until he leaves that place.".

iwh_id: Khaulah bint Hakim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i heard Rasulullah ﷺ saying, "Whosoever alights somewhere dan says: 'A'udhu bikalimat-illahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaqa (Saya berlindung with the Perfect Words of Allah from the evil of what He has created),' nothing will harm him hingga he leaves that place.".

ar:
وعن ابن عمر رضي الله عنهما قال‏:‏ كان رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم  إذا سافر فأقبل الليل قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏ يا أرض ، ربي وربك الله، أعوذ بك من شرك وشر ما فيك ، وشر ما خلق فيك، وشر ما يدب عليك أعوذ بالله من شر أسد وأسود، ومن الحية والعقرب، ومن ساكن البلد، ومن والد وما ولد‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود‏)‏‏)‏‏
والأسود‏ ‏‏:‏ الشخص، قال الخطابي‏:‏ و‏ ‏ساكن البلد‏ ‏‏:‏ هم الجن الذين هم سكان الأرض ‏.‏ قال‏:‏ والبلد من الأرض‏:‏ ما كان مأوى الحيوان، وإن لم يكن فيه بناء ومنازل ‏.‏ قال‏:‏ ويحتمل أن المراد ‏ ‏ بالوالد‏ ‏ ‏:‏ إبليس ‏ ‏وماولد‏ ‏ ‏:‏ الشياطين

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) would not come (back) to his family by night. He used to come in the morning or in the evening (before dark)..
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would not come (back) to his family by night. He used to come in the morning or in the evening (before dark)..
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ tidak akan come (back) to his family by night. He biasa come in the morning or in the evening (sebelum dark)..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
وعن أنس رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ أقبلنا مع النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم ، حتي إذ كنا بظهر المدينة، قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏آيبون، تائبون ، عابدون ، لربنا حامدون‏"‏ فلم يزل يقول ذلك حتي قدمنا المدينة، ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

"آيبون، تائبون ، عابدون ، لربنا حامدون" فلم يزل يقول ذلك حتي قدمنا المدينة، ((رواه مسلم)).

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: We returned with the Prophet (ﷺ) from a journey; and when we entered the suburbs of Al- Madinah, he (ﷺ) said: "Ayibuna, ta'ibuna, 'abiduna, li-Rabbina hamidun (We are returning in safety, turning to our Rubb, worshipping Him and praising Him)." He (ﷺ) continued repeating these words till we entered Al-Madinah..
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that we returned with the Prophet ﷺ from a journey; and when we entered the suburbs of Al- Madinah, he (ﷺ) said: "Ayibuna, ta'ibuna, 'abiduna, li-Rabbina hamidun (We are returning in safety, turning to our Rubb, worshipping Him and praising Him)." He (ﷺ) continued repeating these words till we entered Al-Madinah..
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa we returned with Nabi ﷺ dari journey; dan ketika kami entered the suburbs of Al- Madinah, he (ﷺ) berkata: "Ayibuna, ta'ibuna, 'abiduna, li-Rabbina hamidun (We are returning in safety, turning to our Rubb, worshipping Him dan praising Him)." He (ﷺ) continued repeating these words till we entered Al-Madinah..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: عن كعب بن مالك رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  كان إذا قدم من سفر بدأ بالمسجد فركع فيه ركعتين ‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ka'b bin Malik (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Whenever the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) returned from a journey, he would proceed straight to the mosque and perform two Rak'ah of (optional) prayer..
iwh_en: Ka'b bin Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that whenever the Messenger of Allah ﷺ returned from a journey, he would proceed straight to the mosque and perform two Rak'ah of (optional) prayer..
iwh_id: Ka'b bin Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa setiap kali Rasulullah ﷺ returned dari journey, ia proceed straight kepada masjid dan perform two Rak'ah of (optional) shalat..

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar:
عن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ‏:‏ ‏
"‏لا يحل لامرأة تؤمن بالله واليوم الآخر تسافر مسيرة يوم وليلة إلا مع ذي محرم عليها‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "It is not permissible for a woman who believes in Allah and the Last Day to make a journey of one day and night unless she is accompanied by a Mahram (husband or any other relative to whom she is prohibited to marry).".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "It is not permissible for a woman who believes in Allah and the Last Day to make a journey of one day and night unless she is accompanied by a Mahram (husband or any other relative to whom she is prohibited to marry).".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "It bukan permissible for seorang perempuan who believes in Allah dan the Last Day to membuat a journey of one day dan night kecuali jika she is accompanied by a Mahram (husband or any other relative to whom she is prohibited to menikah).".

chapter_en: The Book of Etiquette of Traveling
ar: وعن ابن عباس رضي الله عنهما أنه سمع النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏لا يخلون رجل بامرأة إلا ومعها ذو محرم، ولا تسافر المرأة إلا مع ذي محرم‏"‏ فقال له رجل‏:‏ يا رسول الله إن امرأتي خرجت حاجّة، وإني اكتتبت في غزوة كذا وكذا‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏انطلق فحج مع امرأتك‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن ابن عباس رضي الله عنهما أنه سمع النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول: "لا يخلون رجل بامرأة إلا ومعها ذو محرم، ولا تسافر المرأة إلا مع ذي محرم" فقال له رجل: يا رسول الله إن امرأتي خرجت حاجّة، وإني اكتتبت في غزوة كذا وكذا؟ قال: "انطلق فحج مع امرأتك" ((متفق عليه)).
isnad_ar: وعن ابن عباس رضي الله عنهما أنه سمع النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول: "لا يخلون رجل بامرأة إلا ومعها ذو محرم
matn_ar: ولا تسافر المرأة إلا مع ذي محرم" فقال له رجل: يا رسول الله إن امرأتي خرجت حاجّة، وإني اكتتبت في غزوة كذا وكذا؟ قال: "انطلق فحج مع امرأتك" ((متفق عليه)).
matn_ar_clean: ولا تسافر المرأة إلا مع ذي محرم" فقال له رجل: يا رسول الله إن امرأتي خرجت حاجة، وإني اكتتبت في غزوة كذا وكذا؟ قال: "انطلق فحج مع امرأتك" ((متفق عليه)).
sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No man must not be alone with a woman except in the presence of her (Mahram). No woman should travel except in company of a (Mahram)." A man said: "O Messenger of Allah! I have been enrolled for such and such expedition, and my wife left for Hajj." He (ﷺ) said to him, "Go and perform Hajj with your wife.".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "No man must not be alone with a woman except in the presence of her (Mahram). No woman should travel except in company of a (Mahram)." A man said: "O Messenger of Allah! I have been enrolled for such and such expedition, and my wife left for Hajj." He (ﷺ) said to him, "Go and perform Hajj with your wife.".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "No man tidak boleh be alone dengan woman except di presence of her (Mahram). No woman harus travel except in company dari (Mahram)." A man berkata: "O Rasulullah! I have been enrolled for sekian dan sekian expedition, and my wife meninggalkan for Hajj." He (ﷺ) bersabda kepadanya,, "Go dan perform Hajj with your wife.".
